sections.heading: Alert Conditions Configured by Default
sections.body:
Atlas configures the following alert conditions by default to help you
ensure dedicated cluster availability and prevent the cluster from going down due
to disk exhaustion on the primary node and to maintain read availability.

- [Writes have been blocked on your cluster due to critically low disk space](<Writes have been blocked on your cluster due to critically low disk space>)
  occurs when the percentage of used disk has exceeded [write-blocking-thresholds](write-blocking-thresholds),
  and Atlas has [write-blocking behavior](cluster-blocking-writes) to maintain read availability.

  You can view disk metrics through [Real-Time Performance Panel](real-time-metrics-status-tab)
  and review [MongoDB process metrics](review-mongodb-process-metrics).

- [Writes have been unblocked on your cluster](<Writes have been unblocked on your cluster>)
  occurs when the percentage of used disk on the primary node has fallen below the
  [write-blocking-thresholds](write-blocking-thresholds), and Atlas
  automatically unblocks writes to the cluster. See also [Unblocking Writes](unblocking-writes).

  This is an information-only alert that doesn't require any action on your part.
  To prevent future write-blocking events, we recommend that you monitor your
  cluster's disk usage. To learn more, see [Preventing Write-Blocking](write-blocking-prevention).

sections.heading: Alert Conditions You Can Configure
sections.body:
You can configure the following alert condition in the project-level
[Configure Alert Settings](configure-alerts) page to trigger alerts.

[Disk space % used on Data Partition](<Disk space % used on Data Partition is>) occurs when the
percentage of disk space used on any partition that contains the
MongoDB collection data meets or exceeds a certain threshold.
[default alert](default-alert-settings), the threshold is 90
percent of the cluster's [modify all configuration options](scale-cluster).

sections.heading: Common Triggers
sections.body:
The following common events can trigger `Disk Space % Used` alerts:

- The percentage of used disk has exceeded [write-blocking-thresholds](write-blocking-thresholds),
  and Atlas has [write-blocking behavior](cluster-blocking-writes)
  to maintain read availability.

- The disk is full and [Modify a Cluster](scale-cluster-storage) is not enabled.

- Auto-Expand Storage is enabled, but the disk filled too quickly
  for Atlas to expand the cluster's storage space. This
  can happen during data migration.

- Auto-Expand Storage is enabled, but Atlas cannot expand the
  cluster's storage space because it has reached the maximum storage
  capacity for this cluster. For more information on cluster tier
  limits, see the [cluster configuration options](amazon-aws-configuration-options),
  [cluster configuration options](google-gcp-configuration-options), and
  [cluster configuration options](microsoft-azure-configuration-options)
  instance configuration options.

sections.heading: Fix the Immediate Problem
sections.body:
Consider the following actions to help resolve `Disk Space % Used`
alerts through the Atlas UI or API.**Atlas UI:**Atlas UI* [Modify a Cluster](scale-cluster-storage).

  > **Note:** If the disk fills too quickly, Atlas might not be
  > able to expand the cluster's storage space in time,
  > even if Auto-Expand Storage is enabled.

* Manually increase the **Storage** of this cluster
  in the **Cluster Tier** section of the
  [Modify a Cluster](scale-cluster-storage).**Atlas-api:**Atlas Administration API* [Enable Auto-Expand Storage](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-updategroupcluster) using the
  `autoScaling.diskGBEnabled` parameter.

  > **Note:** If the disk fills too quickly, Atlas might not be
  > able to expand the cluster's storage space in time,
  > even if Auto-Expand Storage is enabled.

* [Increase the storage of this cluster](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-updategroupcluster) using the
  `diskSizeGB` parameter.

  > **Note:** For **Low CPU** and **General** clusters, the
  > UI displays **GB**, but the `diskSizeGB` parameter
  > value is in gibibytes (GiB).

sections.heading: Implement a Long-Term Solution
sections.body:
Consider the following long-term solutions to help resolve
`Disk Space % Used` alerts through the Atlas UI or API.**Atlas UI:**Atlas UIIf you have reached the maximum storage capacity for this
cluster, scale to a larger cluster tier in the
**Cluster Tier** section of the
[Modify the Cluster Tier](scale-cluster-instance).**Atlas-api:**Atlas Administration APIIf you have reached the maximum storage capacity for this
cluster, [specify a larger cluster tier](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-updategroupcluster) using the
`replicationSpecs[n].regionConfigs[m].electableSpecs.instanceSize`
parameter.

sections.heading: Monitor Your Progress
sections.body:
You can observe a high percentage of used disk space in the following
ways:

- In the cluster [performance metrics](monitor-cluster-metrics), the
  **Disk Usage** graph displays the used disk space in
  yellow. This indicates that the used disk space is approximately
  70 percent of the Atlas cluster's [Modify the Cluster Tier](scale-cluster-instance).

- Atlas nodes are unhealthy, because the
  used disk space reached the cluster's
  [Modify the Cluster Tier](scale-cluster-instance).

- The cluster [write-blocking behavior](cluster-blocking-writes) and connections from the client.
